title: Less Is More: Why Audio on SoundCloud Looks Different
excerpt: Last year, SoundCloud upgraded its AAC encoder for the first time in over a decade. The new one (Fraunhofer’s libfdk_aac) delivers higher perceptual audio quality across every metric we track. But if you look at a spectrogram, you may notice something unexpected. Here’s the scenario: you upload a lossless master. You download your track from SoundCloud and open it in a spectrum analyser. There’s a hard shelf at 17 kHz where there used to be energy all the way to 20 kHz. The old encoder kept…
content_html:
Last year, SoundCloud upgraded its AAC encoder for the first time in over a decade. The new one (Fraunhofer’s libfdk_aac) delivers higher perceptual audio quality across every metric we track.

But if you look at a spectrogram, you may notice something unexpected.

Here’s the scenario: you upload a lossless master. You download your track from SoundCloud and open it in a spectrum analyser. There’s a hard shelf at 17 kHz where there used to be energy all the way to 20 kHz. The old encoder kept those frequencies. The new one removes them.

It looks like a downgrade. It’s actually the opposite. Here’s why.

Lossless vs lossy

A lossless file (WAV, FLAC) keeps every sample intact. What goes in comes out, bit for bit. No decisions, no compromises.

Lossy compression (AAC, MP3, Ogg) is a different game. The encoder has to throw something away to hit a target file size. The question isn’t whether it loses information. It will. The question is what it chooses to lose.

Make Every Bit Count

When SoundCloud compresses your upload to AAC, the encoder gets a fixed number of bits per chunk of audio. It splits those across frequency bands. More bits per band means a more faithful reproduction. Fewer means more distortion.

There are never enough bits for everything. The encoder has to pick where to spend them.

Play with the sliders. As the bitrate drops, the quantized signal drifts further from the original frequency distribution. But pull the low-pass filter left and something interesting happens: the encoder gives up the top frequencies and everything else gets sharper.

That’s the tradeoff: drop what you can barely hear, get a cleaner signal where you can.

Why the Upper Frequencies?

Human hearing isn’t flat. We’re most sensitive between 2 and 5 kHz. Sensitivity decreases significantly at higher frequencies, and most adults cannot reliably perceive tones above 17 kHz, even under ideal listening conditions.

Because of this, the highest frequencies offer one of the most efficient opportunities for bitrate optimization. A small reduction at the very top of the spectrum allows the encoder to improve accuracy across a much broader range of audible content.

While a spectrogram treats every frequency equally, our ears do not. The encoder is designed around how people hear, not how graphs look.

The encoder cuts where it costs the least.

You could ask why it doesn’t cut bass instead. Low frequencies take very few bits to encode, and you feel them as much as hear them. The top of the spectrum is the cheapest sacrifice by far.

What this looks like on real audio

Here’s a pop mix run through the old and new encoders. This plot shows the difference between the encoded audio and the original. Red means energy was removed, blue means the encoder added something that wasn’t there (artifacts).

The new encoder (right) makes one clear sacrifice: a red band at the top where it deliberately cut. Below that, almost nothing. The old encoder (left) is a different story: speckled red through the upper mids (signal lost in the range you hear best) and blue patches in the low end (artifacts the encoder invented). It’s fighting everywhere at once.

Here’s the same story as a bar chart, comparing the same encoder with and without its low-pass filter:

With the filter off (red), error rate is higher across the mid-range, right where your ears are sharpest. With the filter on (blue), those bands get more bits and the error drops. The filter is doing exactly what it should: trading the indiscernible top for a cleaner middle.

But what about 256 kbps?

This is a fair question. At 256 kbps there are nearly enough bits for everything. You’d expect full bandwidth to work fine. And it almost does.

But “almost” still leaves the encoder making hard choices in the mid-range on complex passages. The encoder we use (libfdk_aac, Fraunhofer’s implementation) is conservative: even at 256 kbps it applies a gentle rolloff around 17 kHz. The improvement is smaller than at lower bitrates, but it’s still measurable.

Is this the right call at 256 kbps? Genuinely debatable. But it’s not arbitrary. Fraunhofer validated these cutoff points with extensive listening tests over decades. The threshold exists because trained listeners couldn’t reliably tell the difference, and the freed bits made the rest of the signal measurably better.

Listen and Decide

We can show you graphs all day. Here’s a blind test instead. Three clips of the same audio. The original and two encoded at 256 kbps: one with the low-pass filter, one without. Can you tell which is which? Which has the low-pass filter applied?

The bottom line

Your track isn’t broken. It just sounds better than it looks.

sentences.zh: 旧的编码器保留了这些频率。

sentences.en: The new one removes them.

blocks.html_en: Lossless vs lossy

sentences.en: Lossless vs lossy

sentences.en: A lossless file (WAV, FLAC) keeps every sample intact.
sentences.zh: 无损文件（WAV、FLAC）使每个样本保持完整。

sentences.en: What goes in comes out, bit for bit.
sentences.zh: 一点一滴地进来，就出来了。

sentences.en: No decisions, no compromises.
sentences.zh: 没有决定，没有妥协。

blocks.html_en: Lossy compression (AAC, MP3, Ogg) is a different game. The encoder has to throw something away to hit a target file size. The question isn’t whether it loses information. It will. The question is what it chooses to lose.

sentences.en: Lossy compression (AAC, MP3, Ogg) is a different game.
sentences.zh: 有损压缩（AAC、MP3、Ogg）是一种不同的游戏。

sentences.en: The encoder has to throw something away to hit a target file size.
sentences.zh: 编码器必须丢弃一些东西才能达到目标文件大小。

sentences.en: The question isn’t whether it loses information.
sentences.zh: 问题不在于它是否会丢失信息。

sentences.en: It will.
sentences.zh: 会的。

sentences.en: The question is what it chooses to lose.
sentences.zh: 问题是它选择失去什么。

blocks.html_en: Make Every Bit Count

sentences.en: Make Every Bit Count
sentences.zh: 让每一点都发挥作用

blocks.html_en: When SoundCloud compresses your upload to AAC, the encoder gets a fixed number of bits per chunk of audio. It splits those across frequency bands. More bits per band means a more faithful reproduction. Fewer means more distortion.

sentences.en: When SoundCloud compresses your upload to AAC, the encoder gets a fixed number of bits per chunk of audio.
sentences.zh: 当 SoundCloud 将您上传的内容压缩为 AAC 时，编码器会获得每块音频的固定位数。

sentences.en: It splits those across frequency bands.
sentences.zh: 它将这些划分到各个频段。

sentences.en: More bits per band means a more faithful reproduction.
sentences.zh: 每频段更多位数意味着更忠实的再现。

sentences.en: Fewer means more distortion.
sentences.zh: 越少意味着失真越大。

blocks.html_en: There are never enough bits for everything. The encoder has to pick where to spend them.

sentences.en: There are never enough bits for everything.
sentences.zh: 永远没有足够的位来容纳所有的东西。

sentences.en: The encoder has to pick where to spend them.
sentences.zh: 编码器必须选择将它们花在哪里。

blocks.html_en: Play with the sliders. As the bitrate drops, the quantized signal drifts further from the original frequency distribution. But pull the low-pass filter left and something interesting happens: the encoder gives up the top frequencies and everything else gets sharper.

sentences.en: Play with the sliders.
sentences.zh: 玩滑块。

sentences.en: As the bitrate drops, the quantized signal drifts further from the original frequency distribution.
sentences.zh: 随着比特率下降，量化信号进一步偏离原始频率分布。

sentences.en: But pull the low-pass filter left and something interesting happens: the encoder gives up the top frequencies and everything else gets sharper.
sentences.zh: 但是将低通滤波器向左拉，有趣的事情发生了：编码器放弃了最高频率，其他一切都变得更加清晰。

blocks.html_en: That’s the tradeoff: drop what you can barely hear, get a cleaner signal where you can.

sentences.en: That’s the tradeoff: drop what you can barely hear, get a cleaner signal where you can.
sentences.zh: 这就是权衡：放弃你几乎听不到的声音，尽可能获得更清晰的信号。

blocks.html_en: Why the Upper Frequencies?

sentences.en: Why the Upper Frequencies?
sentences.zh: 为什么是高频？

blocks.html_en: Human hearing isn’t flat. We’re most sensitive between 2 and 5 kHz. Sensitivity decreases significantly at higher frequencies, and most adults cannot reliably perceive tones above 17 kHz, even under ideal listening conditions.

sentences.en: Human hearing isn’t flat.
sentences.zh: 人类的听觉并不是平坦的。

sentences.en: We’re most sensitive between 2 and 5 kHz.
sentences.zh: 我们在 2 到 5 kHz 之间最敏感。

sentences.en: Sensitivity decreases significantly at higher frequencies, and most adults cannot reliably perceive tones above 17 kHz, even under ideal listening conditions.
sentences.zh: 在较高频率下，灵敏度会显着降低，即使在理想的聆听条件下，大多数成年人也无法可靠地感知 17 kHz 以上的音调。

blocks.html_en: Because of this, the highest frequencies offer one of the most efficient opportunities for bitrate optimization. A small reduction at the very top of the spectrum allows the encoder to improve accuracy across a much broader range of audible content.

sentences.en: Because of this, the highest frequencies offer one of the most efficient opportunities for bitrate optimization.
sentences.zh: 因此，最高频率为比特率优化提供了最有效的机会之一。

sentences.en: A small reduction at the very top of the spectrum allows the encoder to improve accuracy across a much broader range of audible content.
sentences.zh: 频谱最顶部的小幅减少使编码器能够提高更广泛的音频内容范围内的准确性。

blocks.html_en: While a spectrogram treats every frequency equally, our ears do not. The encoder is designed around how people hear, not how graphs look.

sentences.en: While a spectrogram treats every frequency equally, our ears do not.
sentences.zh: 虽然频谱图平等地对待每个频率，但我们的耳朵却不然。

sentences.en: The encoder is designed around how people hear, not how graphs look.
sentences.zh: 编码器是围绕人们如何听到的而不是图形的外观而设计的。

blocks.html_en: The encoder cuts where it costs the least.

sentences.en: The encoder cuts where it costs the least.
sentences.zh: 编码器在成本最低的地方进行切割。

blocks.html_en: You could ask why it doesn’t cut bass instead. Low frequencies take very few bits to encode, and you feel them as much as hear them. The top of the spectrum is the cheapest sacrifice by far.

sentences.en: You could ask why it doesn’t cut bass instead.
sentences.zh: 你可能会问为什么它不削弱低音。

sentences.en: Low frequencies take very few bits to encode, and you feel them as much as hear them.
sentences.zh: 低频只需很少的比特即可编码，您感受到的频率与听到的频率一样多。

sentences.en: The top of the spectrum is the cheapest sacrifice by far.
sentences.zh: 最高的牺牲是迄今为止最便宜的牺牲。

blocks.html_en: What this looks like on real audio

sentences.en: What this looks like on real audio
sentences.zh: 这在真实音频中看起来是什么样子

sentences.zh: 这是通过新旧编码器运行的流行混音。

sentences.en: This plot shows the difference between the encoded audio and the original.
sentences.zh: 该图显示了编码音频和原始音频之间的差异。

sentences.en: Red means energy was removed, blue means the encoder added something that wasn’t there (artifacts).
sentences.zh: 红色表示能量被移除，蓝色表示编码器添加了不存在的东西（伪影）。

blocks.html_en: The new encoder (right) makes one clear sacrifice: a red band at the top where it deliberately cut. Below that, almost nothing. The old encoder (left) is a different story: speckled red through the upper mids (signal lost in the range you hear best) and blue patches in the low end (artifacts the encoder invented). It’s fighting everywhere at once.

sentences.en: The new encoder (right) makes one clear sacrifice: a red band at the top where it deliberately cut.
sentences.zh: 新的编码器（右）做出了一个明显的牺牲：在顶部刻意切割的红色条带。

sentences.en: Below that, almost nothing.
sentences.zh: 在此之下，几乎什么都没有。

sentences.en: The old encoder (left) is a different story: speckled red through the upper mids (signal lost in the range you hear best) and blue patches in the low end (artifacts the encoder invented).
sentences.zh: 旧的编码器（左）是一个不同的故事：中高频有红色斑点（信号在您听到最好的范围内丢失），低端有蓝色斑点（编码器发明的伪像）。

sentences.en: It’s fighting everywhere at once.
sentences.zh: 它同时在各处战斗。

blocks.html_en: With the filter off (red), error rate is higher across the mid-range, right where your ears are sharpest. With the filter on (blue), those bands get more bits and the error drops. The filter is doing exactly what it should: trading the indiscernible top for a cleaner middle.

sentences.en: With the filter off (red), error rate is higher across the mid-range, right where your ears are sharpest.
sentences.zh: 过滤器关闭（红色）时，中频范围内的错误率较高，也就是您的耳朵最敏锐的地方。

sentences.en: With the filter on (blue), those bands get more bits and the error drops.
sentences.zh: 当滤波器打开（蓝色）时，这些频段获得更多比特并且误差下降。

sentences.en: The filter is doing exactly what it should: trading the indiscernible top for a cleaner middle.
sentences.zh: 过滤器正在做它应该做的事情：将难以辨别的顶部换成更干净的中间。

blocks.html_en: But what about 256 kbps?

sentences.en: But what about 256 kbps?
sentences.zh: 但是 256 kbps 又如何呢？

blocks.html_en: This is a fair question. At 256 kbps there are nearly enough bits for everything. You’d expect full bandwidth to work fine. And it almost does.

sentences.en: This is a fair question.
sentences.zh: 这是一个公平的问题。

sentences.en: At 256 kbps there are nearly enough bits for everything.
sentences.zh: 在 256 kbps 下，几乎有足够的位来处理所有事情。

sentences.en: You’d expect full bandwidth to work fine.
sentences.zh: 您希望全带宽能够正常工作。

sentences.en: And it almost does.
sentences.zh: 几乎确实如此。

blocks.html_en: But “almost” still leaves the encoder making hard choices in the mid-range on complex passages. The encoder we use (libfdk_aac, Fraunhofer’s implementation) is conservative: even at 256 kbps it applies a gentle rolloff around 17 kHz. The improvement is smaller than at lower bitrates, but it’s still measurable.

sentences.zh: 但“几乎”仍然让编码器在复杂段落的中间范围内做出艰难的选择。

sentences.en: The encoder we use (libfdk_aac, Fraunhofer’s implementation) is conservative: even at 256 kbps it applies a gentle rolloff around 17 kHz.
sentences.zh: 我们使用的编码器（libfdk_aac，Fraunhofer 的实现）是保守的：即使在 256 kbps 下，它也会在 17 kHz 左右进行温和的滚降。

sentences.en: The improvement is smaller than at lower bitrates, but it’s still measurable.
sentences.zh: 与较低比特率相比，改进幅度较小，但仍然是可测量的。

blocks.html_en: Is this the right call at 256 kbps? Genuinely debatable. But it’s not arbitrary. Fraunhofer validated these cutoff points with extensive listening tests over decades. The threshold exists because trained listeners couldn’t reliably tell the difference, and the freed bits made the rest of the signal measurably better.

sentences.en: Is this the right call at 256 kbps?
sentences.zh: 这是 256 kbps 的正确调用吗？

sentences.en: Genuinely debatable.
sentences.zh: 确实值得商榷。

sentences.en: But it’s not arbitrary.
sentences.zh: 但这不是任意的。

sentences.en: Fraunhofer validated these cutoff points with extensive listening tests over decades.
sentences.zh: 弗劳恩霍夫通过数十年的广泛听力测试验证了这些截止点。

sentences.en: The threshold exists because trained listeners couldn’t reliably tell the difference, and the freed bits made the rest of the signal measurably better.
sentences.zh: 阈值的存在是因为经过训练的听众无法可靠地辨别差异，而释放的位使信号的其余部分明显更好。

sentences.en: Listen and Decide
sentences.zh: 倾听并决定

blocks.html_en: We can show you graphs all day. Here’s a blind test instead. Three clips of the same audio. The original and two encoded at 256 kbps: one with the low-pass filter, one without. Can you tell which is which? Which has the low-pass filter applied?

sentences.en: The original and two encoded at 256 kbps: one with the low-pass filter, one without.
sentences.zh: 原始的和两种以 256 kbps 编码的：一种带有低通滤波器，一种没有。

sentences.en: Can you tell which is which?
sentences.zh: 你能分辨出哪个是哪个吗？

sentences.en: Which has the low-pass filter applied?
sentences.zh: 哪个应用了低通滤波器？

blocks.html_en: The bottom line

sentences.en: The bottom line
sentences.zh: 底线

blocks.html_en: Your track isn’t broken. It just sounds better than it looks.

sentences.en: Your track isn’t broken.
sentences.zh: 你的轨迹没有中断。

sentences.en: It just sounds better than it looks.
sentences.zh: 它只是听起来比看起来更好。

无损与有损

无损文件（WAV、FLAC）使每个样本保持完整。 一点一滴地进来，就出来了。 没有决定，没有妥协。

有损压缩（AAC、MP3、Ogg）是一种不同的游戏。 编码器必须丢弃一些东西才能达到目标文件大小。 问题不在于它是否会丢失信息。 会的。 问题是它选择失去什么。

让每一点都发挥作用

当 SoundCloud 将您上传的内容压缩为 AAC 时，编码器会获得每块音频的固定位数。 它将这些划分到各个频段。 每频段更多位数意味着更忠实的再现。 越少意味着失真越大。

永远没有足够的位来容纳所有的东西。 编码器必须选择将它们花在哪里。

玩滑块。 随着比特率下降，量化信号进一步偏离原始频率分布。 但是将低通滤波器向左拉，有趣的事情发生了：编码器放弃了最高频率，其他一切都变得更加清晰。

这就是权衡：放弃你几乎听不到的声音，尽可能获得更清晰的信号。

为什么是高频？

人类的听觉并不是平坦的。 我们在 2 到 5 kHz 之间最敏感。 在较高频率下，灵敏度会显着降低，即使在理想的聆听条件下，大多数成年人也无法可靠地感知 17 kHz 以上的音调。

因此，最高频率为比特率优化提供了最有效的机会之一。 频谱最顶部的小幅减少使编码器能够提高更广泛的音频内容范围内的准确性。

虽然频谱图平等地对待每个频率，但我们的耳朵却不然。 编码器是围绕人们如何听到的而不是图形的外观而设计的。

编码器在成本最低的地方进行切割。

你可能会问为什么它不削弱低音。 低频只需很少的比特即可编码，您感受到的频率与听到的频率一样多。 最高的牺牲是迄今为止最便宜的牺牲。

这在真实音频中看起来是什么样子

这是通过新旧编码器运行的流行混音。 该图显示了编码音频和原始音频之间的差异。 红色表示能量被移除，蓝色表示编码器添加了不存在的东西（伪影）。

新的编码器（右）做出了一个明显的牺牲：在顶部刻意切割的红色条带。 在此之下，几乎什么都没有。 旧的编码器（左）是一个不同的故事：中高频有红色斑点（信号在您听到最好的范围内丢失），低端有蓝色斑点（编码器发明的伪像）。 它同时在各处战斗。

这是与条形图相同的故事，比较带有和不带有低通滤波器的相同编码器：

过滤器关闭（红色）时，中频范围内的错误率较高，也就是您的耳朵最敏锐的地方。 当滤波器打开（蓝色）时，这些频段获得更多比特并且误差下降。 过滤器正在做它应该做的事情：将难以辨别的顶部换成更干净的中间。

但是 256 kbps 又如何呢？

这是一个公平的问题。 在 256 kbps 下，几乎有足够的位来处理所有事情。 您希望全带宽能够正常工作。 几乎确实如此。

但“几乎”仍然让编码器在复杂段落的中间范围内做出艰难的选择。 我们使用的编码器（libfdk_aac，Fraunhofer 的实现）是保守的：即使在 256 kbps 下，它也会在 17 kHz 左右进行温和的滚降。 与较低比特率相比，改进幅度较小，但仍然是可测量的。

这是 256 kbps 的正确调用吗？ 确实值得商榷。 但这不是任意的。 弗劳恩霍夫通过数十年的广泛听力测试验证了这些截止点。 阈值的存在是因为经过训练的听众无法可靠地辨别差异，而释放的位使信号的其余部分明显更好。

倾听并决定

我们可以全天向您展示图表。 这是一个盲测。 同一音频的三个剪辑。 原始的和两种以 256 kbps 编码的：一种带有低通滤波器，一种没有。 你能分辨出哪个是哪个吗？ 哪个应用了低通滤波器？

底线

你的轨迹没有中断。 它只是听起来比看起来更好。
